title: carduncellus monspelliensium subsp. araneosus
intro: Carduncellus monspelliensium subsp. araneosus, also known as the hairy cardoon, is a subspecies of the cardoon plant (Cynara carduncellus), a type of thistle-like flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region. The hairy cardoon has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and digestive problems. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and aid in the treatment of gastrointestinal disorders. In herbal preparations, the hairy cardoon is often used in infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, where it is valued for its ability to soothe and heal the skin.
